Bug / Task Description: Iterating a range with `for ... in start..end` (evalForStmt's Range case, pkg/evaluator/evaluator.go) visits one extra value at the upper bound: the end is treated as inclusive rather than exclusive. Loops over `0..n` include the n-th step, and index patterns driven by such loops read one past the intended end. The language treats `..` as half-open everywhere else (Range.Len uses End-Start).
Commit Subject: fix(evaluator): range iteration upper bound is exclusive
